1. Language & Expression.desc: Arabic grammar, morphology, rhetoric, and philology — how the Qurʾān says what it says

2. Text & Transmission.desc: Qirāʾāt, canonical readings, and their interpretive implications — how the text is preserved and transmitted

3. Context & Revelation.desc: Asbāb al-nuzūl, prophetic biography, and historical setting — why and when verses were revealed

4. Prophetic & Early Interpretation.desc: Hadith evidence, companion explanations, and early scholarly tradition — how the Qurʾān was understood earliest

5. Legal Interpretation.desc: Fiqh, legal verses, and differences of opinion — what rulings are derived from the text

6. Legal Methodology.desc: Uṣūl al-fiqh, general vs. specific, literal vs. metaphorical — how rulings are derived from the text

7. Theology & Belief.desc: ʿAqīda, divine attributes, and unseen matters — doctrinal meaning of the Qurʾānic text

8. Abrogation & Inter-Text.desc: Nāsikh wa mansūkh, apparent contradictions, and harmonisation — how verses relate to each other

9. Quranic Sciences.desc: Muḥkam/mutashābih, mushkil al-Qurʾān, thematic structure — internal classifications of the Qurʾān

10. Narrative & Thematic Content.desc: Prophetic narratives, ethical themes, and social guidance — what the Qurʾān talks about

11. Spiritual & Reflective.desc: Tazkiyah, moral lessons, and tadabbur — the inner meaning and transformative dimension

12. Rational & Philosophical.desc: Kalām, philosophical theology, and rational argumentation — intellectual dimensions of interpretation
